notes: NI Symphony Series Brass Ensemble — full orchestral brass section (trumpets, horns, trombones, tuba) recorded at Teldex Studio Berlin. CC 1 (Modulation) cross-fades dynamic layers (pp to ff); CC 11 (Expression) rides the level envelope — automate both simultaneously for convincing orchestral brass. Key switches select articulations (sustain, staccato, sforzando, falls etc. — default C0–B0 range). Brass response is naturally slower than strings; allow time in note timings for the attack to bloom. For sforzando accents, use high CC 1 + CC 11 with a short note and quick CC 11 pull-back.

Expression.description: Expression — output level envelope. Automate for phrasing. Combine with CC 1 for natural brass swells; pull back at phrase ends.

Modulation.description: Dynamics cross-fade — morphs between soft (pp) and powerful (ff) dynamic layers, changing both volume and tone colour. Essential for realistic orchestral brass.

Quick Control 1.description: Ensemble Blend — balance between section weight; lower = leaner soloistic character, higher = full tutti mass

Quick Control 2.description: Mute Amount — straight/cup/harmon mute blend (muted articulations must be loaded; check KS assignments)

Quick Control 3.description: Attack Speed — note speaking time; lower = soft and blossoming, higher = punchy and immediate

Quick Control 4.description: Reverb Amount — built-in reverb / hall tail mix

Quick Control 5.description: Vibrato Depth — natural vibrato intensity (horn players use significant vibrato; trumpets less)

Quick Control 6.description: Growl / Flutter Tongue — flutter-tongue or growl effect depth (when available in loaded articulation)

Quick Control 7.description: Brightness — spectral brightness / harmonic presence of the brass tone

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random timing and tuning variation between players

Stereo Width.description: Stereo image width of the brass section

Close Mic.description: Close microphone level — spot mic, more attack definition
